<content>1: package overlay
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"strings"
5: 
6: 	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
7: 	"github.com/charmbracelet/huh"
8: 	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
9: )
10: 
11: // FormOverlay is a multi-field form overlay backed by huh.Form.
12: type FormOverlay struct {
13: 	form      *huh.Form
14: 	nameVal   string
15: 	descVal   string
16: 	branchVal string
17: 	pathVal   string
18: 	title     string
19: 	submitted bool
20: 	canceled  bool
21: 	width     int
22: 	fieldKeys []string
23: }
24: 
25: // NewFormOverlay creates a form overlay with name and description inputs.
26: func NewFormOverlay(title string, width int) *FormOverlay {
27: 	f := &FormOverlay{
28: 		title:     title,
29: 		width:     width,
30: 		fieldKeys: []string{"name", "desc"},
31: 	}
32: 
33: 	formWidth := width - 6
34: 	if formWidth < 34 {
35: 		formWidth = 34
36: 	}
37: 
38: 	f.form = huh.NewForm(
39: 		huh.NewGroup(
40: 			huh.NewInput().
41: 				Key("name").
42: 				Title("name").
43: 				Value(&f.nameVal),
44: 			huh.NewInput().
45: 				Key("desc").
46: 				Title("description (optional)").
47: 				Value(&f.descVal),
48: 		),
49: 	).
50: 		WithTheme(ThemeRosePine()).
51: 		WithWidth(formWidth).
52: 		WithShowHelp(false).
53: 		WithShowErrors(false)
54: 
55: 	_ = f.form.Init()
56: 
57: 	return f
58: }
59: 
60: // NewSpawnFormOverlay creates a form overlay with name, branch (optional), and path (optional) inputs.
61: func NewSpawnFormOverlay(title string, width int) *FormOverlay {
62: 	f := &FormOverlay{
63: 		title:     title,
64: 		width:     width,
65: 		fieldKeys: []string{"name", "branch", "path"},
66: 	}
67: 
68: 	formWidth := width - 6
69: 	if formWidth < 34 {
70: 		formWidth = 34
71: 	}
72: 
73: 	f.form = huh.NewForm(
74: 		huh.NewGroup(
75: 			huh.NewInput().
76: 				Key("name").
77: 				Title("name").
78: 				Value(&f.nameVal),
79: 			huh.NewInput().
80: 				Key("branch").
81: 				Title("branch (optional)").
82: 				Value(&f.branchVal),
83: 			huh.NewInput().
84: 				Key("path").
85: 				Title("path (optional)").
86: 				Value(&f.pathVal),
87: 		),
88: 	).
89: 		WithTheme(ThemeRosePine()).
90: 		WithWidth(formWidth).
91: 		WithShowHelp(false).
92: 		WithShowErrors(false)
93: 
94: 	_ = f.form.Init()
95: 
96: 	return f
97: }
98: 
99: func (f *FormOverlay) updateForm(msg tea.Msg) {
100: 	updated, _ := f.form.Update(msg)
101: 	if form, ok := updated.(*huh.Form); ok {
102: 		f.form = form
103: 	}
104: }
105: 
106: func (f *FormOverlay) focusedKey() string {
107: 	field := f.form.GetFocusedField()
108: 	if field == nil {
109: 		return ""
110: 	}
111: 	return field.GetKey()
112: }
113: 
114: // Name returns the name field value.
115: func (f *FormOverlay) Name() string {
116: 	return strings.TrimSpace(f.nameVal)
117: }
118: 
119: // Description returns the description field value.
120: func (f *FormOverlay) Description() string {
121: 	return strings.TrimSpace(f.descVal)
122: }
123: 
124: // Branch returns the branch field value.
125: func (f *FormOverlay) Branch() string {
126: 	return strings.TrimSpace(f.branchVal)
127: }
128: 
129: // WorkPath returns the path field value.
130: func (f *FormOverlay) WorkPath() string {
131: 	return strings.TrimSpace(f.pathVal)
132: }
133: 
134: // HandleKey implements Overlay. Processes a key event and returns a Result.
135: func (f *FormOverlay) HandleKey(msg tea.KeyMsg) Result {
136: 	switch msg.Type {
137: 	case tea.KeyEsc:
138: 		f.canceled = true
139: 		return Result{Dismissed: true}
140: 
141: 	case tea.KeyEnter:
142: 		if strings.TrimSpace(f.nameVal) == "" {
143: 			return Result{}
144: 		}
145: 		f.submitted = true
146: 		return Result{Dismissed: true, Submitted: true, Value: f.Name()}
147: 
148: 	case tea.KeyTab, tea.KeyDown:
149: 		focused := f.focusedKey()
150: 		if len(f.fieldKeys) > 0 && focused == f.fieldKeys[len(f.fieldKeys)-1] {
151: 			for i := 0; i < len(f.fieldKeys)-1; i++ {
152: 				f.updateForm(huh.PrevField())
153: 			}
154: 			return Result{}
155: 		}
156: 		f.updateForm(huh.NextField())
157: 		return Result{}
158: 
159: 	case tea.KeyShiftTab, tea.KeyUp:
160: 		focused := f.focusedKey()
161: 		if len(f.fieldKeys) > 0 && focused == f.fieldKeys[0] {
162: 			for i := 0; i < len(f.fieldKeys)-1; i++ {
163: 				f.updateForm(huh.NextField())
164: 			}
165: 			return Result{}
166: 		}
167: 		f.updateForm(huh.PrevField())
168: 		return Result{}
169: 
170: 	default:
171: 		f.updateForm(msg)
172: 		return Result{}
173: 	}
174: }
175: 
176: // View implements Overlay. Returns the rendered overlay string.
177: func (f *FormOverlay) View() string {
178: 	w := f.width
179: 	if w < 40 {
180: 		w = 40
181: 	}
182: 
183: 	titleStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
184: 		Foreground(colorIris).
185: 		Bold(true).
186: 		MarginBottom(1)
187: 
188: 	hintStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
189: 		Foreground(colorMuted).
190: 		MarginTop(1)
191: 
192: 	content := titleStyle.Render(f.title) + "\n"
193: 	content += f.form.View() + "\n"
194: 	content += hintStyle.Render("tab/↑↓ navigate · enter create")
195: 
196: 	style := lipgloss.NewStyle().
197: 		Border(lipgloss.DoubleBorder()).
198: 		BorderForeground(colorIris).
199: 		Padding(1, 2).
200: 		Width(w)
201: 
202: 	return style.Render(content)
203: }
204: 
205: // SetSize implements Overlay. Updates the available width for the overlay.
206: func (f *FormOverlay) SetSize(w, h int) {
207: 	f.width = w
208: }

(End of file - total 208 lines)

<content>1: package overlay
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"fmt"
5: 	"strings"
6: 
7: 	tea "github.com/charmbracelet/bubbletea"
8: 	"github.com/charmbracelet/lipgloss"
9: )
10: 
11: // PermissionChoice mirrors tmux.PermissionChoice to avoid import cycle.
12: // Ordering matches opencode's left-to-right menu navigation and tmux.PermissionChoice,
13: // so app_input.go can cast directly without a mapping switch.
14: type PermissionChoice int
15: 
16: const (
17: 	PermissionAllowOnce   PermissionChoice = iota // 0 — opencode's default cursor position
18: 	PermissionAllowAlways                         // 1 — one Right arrow from default
19: 	PermissionReject                              // 2 — two Right arrows from default
20: )
21: 
22: var permissionChoiceLabels = []string{"allow once", "allow always", "reject"}
23: 
24: // PermissionOverlay shows a three-choice modal for opencode permission prompts.
25: type PermissionOverlay struct {
26: 	instanceTitle string
27: 	description   string
28: 	pattern       string
29: 	selectedIdx   int
30: 	confirmed     bool
31: 	width         int
32: }
33: 
34: // NewPermissionOverlay creates a permission overlay with extracted prompt data.
35: func NewPermissionOverlay(instanceTitle, description, pattern string) *PermissionOverlay {
36: 	return &PermissionOverlay{
37: 		instanceTitle: instanceTitle,
38: 		description:   description,
39: 		pattern:       pattern,
40: 		selectedIdx:   0, // default to "allow once" (opencode's default cursor position)
41: 		width:         50,
42: 	}
43: }
44: 
45: // Choice returns the selected permission choice.
46: func (p *PermissionOverlay) Choice() PermissionChoice {
47: 	return PermissionChoice(p.selectedIdx)
48: }
49: 
50: // IsConfirmed returns true if the user pressed Enter.
51: func (p *PermissionOverlay) IsConfirmed() bool {
52: 	return p.confirmed
53: }
54: 
55: // Pattern returns the permission pattern string extracted from the agent pane.
56: // Use this on confirm instead of re-parsing CachedContent, which may have changed.
57: func (p *PermissionOverlay) Pattern() string {
58: 	return p.pattern
59: }
60: 
61: // Description returns the permission description shown in the overlay.
62: func (p *PermissionOverlay) Description() string {
63: 	return p.description
64: }
65: 
66: // render draws the permission overlay.
67: func (p *PermissionOverlay) render() string {
68: 	borderStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
69: 		Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
70: 		BorderForeground(colorGold).
71: 		Padding(1, 2).
72: 		Width(p.width)
73: 
74: 	titleStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
75: 		Bold(true).
76: 		Foreground(colorGold)
77: 
78: 	descStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
79: 		Foreground(colorText)
80: 
81: 	patternStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
82: 		Foreground(colorMuted)
83: 
84: 	hintStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
85: 		Foreground(colorMuted)
86: 
87: 	selectedStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
88: 		Background(colorFoam).
89: 		Foreground(colorBase).
90: 		Padding(0, 1)
91: 
92: 	normalStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
93: 		Foreground(colorText).
94: 		Padding(0, 1)
95: 
96: 	var b strings.Builder
97: 	b.WriteString(titleStyle.Render("△ permission required"))
98: 	b.WriteString("\n")
99: 	b.WriteString(descStyle.Render(p.description))
100: 	if p.pattern != "" {
101: 		b.WriteString("\n")
102: 		b.WriteString(patternStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("pattern: %s", p.pattern)))
103: 	}
104: 	if p.instanceTitle != "" {
105: 		b.WriteString("\n")
106: 		b.WriteString(patternStyle.Render(fmt.Sprintf("instance: %s", p.instanceTitle)))
107: 	}
108: 	b.WriteString("\n\n")
109: 
110: 	// Render choices horizontally
111: 	var choices []string
112: 	for i, label := range permissionChoiceLabels {
113: 		if i == p.selectedIdx {
114: 			choices = append(choices, selectedStyle.Render("▸ "+label))
115: 		} else {
116: 			choices = append(choices, normalStyle.Render("  "+label))
117: 		}
118: 	}
119: 	b.WriteString(lipgloss.JoinHorizontal(lipgloss.Center, choices...))
120: 	b.WriteString("\n\n")
121: 	b.WriteString(hintStyle.Render("←→ select · enter confirm · esc dismiss"))
122: 
123: 	return borderStyle.Render(b.String())
124: }
125: 
126: // permissionActionLabels maps selectedIdx to the action string returned by HandleKey.
127: var permissionActionLabels = []string{"allow_once", "allow_always", "reject"}
128: 
129: // HandleKey implements Overlay. Processes a key event and returns a Result.
130: func (p *PermissionOverlay) HandleKey(msg tea.KeyMsg) Result {
131: 	switch msg.Type {
132: 	case tea.KeyLeft:
133: 		if p.selectedIdx > 0 {
134: 			p.selectedIdx--
135: 		}
136: 		return Result{}
137: 	case tea.KeyRight:
138: 		if p.selectedIdx < len(permissionChoiceLabels)-1 {
139: 			p.selectedIdx++
140: 		}
141: 		return Result{}
142: 	case tea.KeyEnter:
143: 		p.confirmed = true
144: 		action := permissionActionLabels[p.selectedIdx]
145: 		return Result{Dismissed: true, Submitted: true, Action: action}
146: 	case tea.KeyEsc:
147: 		return Result{Dismissed: true}
148: 	}
149: 	return Result{}
150: }
151: 
152: // View implements Overlay. Returns the rendered overlay string.
153: func (p *PermissionOverlay) View() string {
154: 	return p.render()
155: }
156: 
157: // SetSize implements Overlay. Updates the available dimensions for the overlay.
158: func (p *PermissionOverlay) SetSize(w, h int) {
159: 	p.width = w
160: }

12: // TmuxBrowserItem holds metadata for a single tmux session (managed or orphaned).
13: type TmuxBrowserItem struct {
14: 	Name      string
15: 	Title     string
16: 	Created   time.Time
17: 	Windows   int
18: 	Attached  bool
19: 	Width     int
20: 	Height    int

22: 	TaskFile  string // task filename (managed only)
23: 	AgentType string // "planner"/"coder"/"reviewer" (managed only)

26: 
27: var browserBorderStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
28: 	Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
29: 	BorderForeground(colorIris).
30: 	Padding(1, 2)
31: 
32: var browserTitleStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
33: 	Bold(true).
34: 	Foreground(colorIris).
35: 	MarginBottom(1)
36: 
37: var browserSearchStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
38: 	Border(lipgloss.RoundedBorder()).
39: 	BorderForeground(colorFoam).
40: 	Padding(0, 1).
41: 	MarginBottom(1)
42: 
43: var browserItemStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
44: 	Padding(0, 1).
45: 	Foreground(colorText)
46: 
47: var browserSelectedStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
48: 	Padding(0, 1).
49: 	Background(colorFoam).
50: 	Foreground(colorBase)
51: 
52: var browserMutedStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
53: 	Foreground(colorMuted)
54: 
55: var browserHintStyle = lipgloss.NewStyle().
56: 	Foreground(colorMuted).
57: 	MarginTop(1)
58: 
59: // TmuxBrowserOverlay shows orphaned tmux sessions with kill/adopt/attach actions.
60: type TmuxBrowserOverlay struct {
61: 	sessions    []TmuxBrowserItem
62: 	filtered    []int // indices into sessions
63: 	selectedIdx int
64: 	searchQuery string
65: 	width       int
66: }
67: 
68: // NewTmuxBrowserOverlay creates a browser overlay from discovered orphan sessions.
69: func NewTmuxBrowserOverlay(items []TmuxBrowserItem) *TmuxBrowserOverlay {
70: 	b := &TmuxBrowserOverlay{
71: 		sessions: items,
72: 		width:    56,
73: 	}
74: 	b.applyFilter()
75: 	return b
76: }
77: 
78: func (b *TmuxBrowserOverlay) applyFilter() {
79: 	b.filtered = nil
80: 	query := strings.ToLower(b.searchQuery)
81: 	for i, item := range b.sessions {
82: 		if query == "" || strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(item.Title), query) {
83: 			b.filtered = append(b.filtered, i)
84: 		}
85: 	}
86: 	if b.selectedIdx >= len(b.filtered) {
87: 		b.selectedIdx = len(b.filtered) - 1
88: 	}
89: 	if b.selectedIdx < 0 {
90: 		b.selectedIdx = 0
91: 	}
92: }
93: 
94: // SelectedItem returns the currently highlighted session, or a zero value if empty.
95: func (b *TmuxBrowserOverlay) SelectedItem() TmuxBrowserItem {
96: 	if len(b.filtered) == 0 || b.selectedIdx >= len(b.filtered) {
97: 		return TmuxBrowserItem{}
98: 	}
99: 	return b.sessions[b.filtered[b.selectedIdx]]
100: }
101: 
102: // RemoveSelected removes the currently selected item from the list.
103: func (b *TmuxBrowserOverlay) RemoveSelected() {
104: 	if len(b.filtered) == 0 || b.selectedIdx >= len(b.filtered) {
105: 		return
106: 	}
107: 	idx := b.filtered[b.selectedIdx]
108: 	b.sessions = append(b.sessions[:idx], b.sessions[idx+1:]...)
109: 	b.applyFilter()
110: }
111: 
112: // IsEmpty returns true if there are no sessions to display.
113: func (b *TmuxBrowserOverlay) IsEmpty() bool {
114: 	return len(b.sessions) == 0
115: }
116: 
117: // render draws the browser overlay.
118: func (b *TmuxBrowserOverlay) render() string {
119: 	var s strings.Builder
120: 
121: 	s.WriteString(browserTitleStyle.Render("tmux sessions"))
122: 	s.WriteString("\n")
123: 
124: 	// Search bar
125: 	innerWidth := b.width - 8
126: 	if innerWidth < 10 {
127: 		innerWidth = 10
128: 	}
129: 	searchText := b.searchQuery
130: 	if searchText == "" {
131: 		searchText = browserMutedStyle.Render("\uf002 type to filter...")
132: 	}
133: 	s.WriteString(browserSearchStyle.Width(innerWidth).Render(searchText))
134: 	s.WriteString("\n")
135: 
136: 	// Items
137: 	if len(b.filtered) == 0 {
138: 		s.WriteString(browserMutedStyle.Render("  no sessions"))
139: 		s.WriteString("\n")
140: 	} else {
141: 		for i, idx := range b.filtered {
142: 			item := b.sessions[idx]
143: 			age := relativeTime(item.Created)
144: 			dims := fmt.Sprintf("%d×%d", item.Width, item.Height)
145: 
146: 			attachedIndicator := "  "
147: 			if item.Attached {
148: 				attachedIndicator = "● "
149: 			}
150: 
151: 			// Badge for managed items
152: 			badge := ""
153: 			if item.Managed {
154: 				badgeText := "managed"
155: 				if item.AgentType != "" {
156: 					badgeText = item.AgentType
157: 				}
158: 				badge = browserMutedStyle.Render(" [" + badgeText + "]")
159: 			}
160: 
161: 			label := fmt.Sprintf("%-28s %8s %s%s",
162: 				truncateStr(item.Title, 28), age, attachedIndicator, dims) + badge
163: 
164: 			if i == b.selectedIdx {
165: 				s.WriteString(browserSelectedStyle.Width(innerWidth).Render("▸ " + label))
166: 			} else {
167: 				s.WriteString(browserItemStyle.Width(innerWidth).Render("  " + label))
168: 			}
169: 			s.WriteString("\n")
170: 		}
171: 	}
172: 
173: 	hint := "↑↓ navigate · k kill · o attach · esc close"
174: 	if len(b.filtered) > 0 && !b.SelectedItem().Managed {
175: 		hint = "↑↓ navigate · k kill · a adopt · o attach · esc close"
176: 	}
177: 	s.WriteString(browserHintStyle.Render(hint))
178: 
179: 	return browserBorderStyle.Width(b.width).Render(s.String())
180: }
181: 
182: // SetSize updates the overlay width.
183: func (b *TmuxBrowserOverlay) SetSize(width, height int) {
184: 	b.width = width
185: }
186: 
187: // HandleKey implements Overlay. Processes a key event and returns a Result.
188: func (b *TmuxBrowserOverlay) HandleKey(msg tea.KeyMsg) Result {
189: 	switch msg.Type {
190: 	case tea.KeyEsc:
191: 		if b.searchQuery != "" {
192: 			b.searchQuery = ""
193: 			b.applyFilter()
194: 			return Result{}
195: 		}
196: 		return Result{Dismissed: true}
197: 	case tea.KeyEnter:
198: 		if len(b.filtered) > 0 {
199: 			return Result{Dismissed: true, Action: "attach"}
200: 		}
201: 		return Result{}
202: 	case tea.KeyUp:
203: 		if b.selectedIdx > 0 {
204: 			b.selectedIdx--
205: 		}
206: 		return Result{}
207: 	case tea.KeyDown:
208: 		if b.selectedIdx < len(b.filtered)-1 {
209: 			b.selectedIdx++
210: 		}
211: 		return Result{}
212: 	case tea.KeyBackspace:
213: 		if len(b.searchQuery) > 0 {
214: 			runes := []rune(b.searchQuery)
215: 			b.searchQuery = string(runes[:len(runes)-1])
216: 			b.applyFilter()
217: 		}
218: 		return Result{}
219: 	case tea.KeyRunes:
220: 		r := string(msg.Runes)
221: 		// Action keys only fire when search is empty
222: 		if b.searchQuery == "" {
223: 			switch r {
224: 			case "k":
225: 				if len(b.filtered) > 0 {
226: 					return Result{Dismissed: true, Action: "kill"}
227: 				}
228: 				return Result{}
229: 			case "a":
230: 				if len(b.filtered) > 0 && !b.SelectedItem().Managed {
231: 					return Result{Dismissed: true, Action: "adopt"}
232: 				}
233: 				return Result{}
234: 			case "o":
235: 				if len(b.filtered) > 0 {
236: 					return Result{Dismissed: true, Action: "attach"}
237: 				}
238: 				return Result{}
239: 			}
240: 		}
241: 		// All other runes type into search
242: 		b.searchQuery += r
243: 		b.applyFilter()
244: 		return Result{}
245: 	}
246: 	return Result{}
247: }
248: 
249: // View implements Overlay. Returns the rendered overlay string.
250: func (b *TmuxBrowserOverlay) View() string {
251: 	return b.render()
252: }
253: 
254: // truncateStr truncates s to maxLen runes, appending "…" if truncated.
255: func truncateStr(s string, maxLen int) string {
256: 	runes := []rune(s)
257: 	if len(runes) <= maxLen {
258: 		return s
259: 	}
260: 	return string(runes[:maxLen-1]) + "…"
261: }
262: 
263: // relativeTime returns a human-readable relative time string.
264: func relativeTime(t time.Time) string {
265: 	if t.IsZero() {
266: 		return ""
267: 	}
268: 	d := time.Since(t)
269: 	switch {
270: 	case d < time.Minute:
271: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%ds ago", int(d.Seconds()))
272: 	case d < time.Hour:
273: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%dm ago", int(d.Minutes()))
274: 	case d < 24*time.Hour:
275: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%dh ago", int(d.Hours()))
276: 	default:
277: 		return fmt.Sprintf("%dd ago", int(d.Hours()/24))
278: 	}
279: }

12: func TestTmuxBrowserOverlay_Basic(t *testing.T) {
13: 	items := []TmuxBrowserItem{
14: 		{Name: "kas_foo", Title: "foo", Created: time.Now().Add(-10 * time.Minute), Width: 80, Height: 24},
15: 		{Name: "kas_bar", Title: "bar", Created: time.Now().Add(-3 * time.Hour), Width: 120, Height: 40, Attached: true},
16: 	}
17: 
18: 	b := NewTmuxBrowserOverlay(items)
19: 	require.NotNil(t, b)
20: 
21: 	// View should not panic and should contain session titles
22: 	rendered := b.View()
23: 	assert.Contains(t, rendered, "foo")
24: 	assert.Contains(t, rendered, "bar")
25: }

27: func TestTmuxBrowserOverlay_Navigation(t *testing.T) {
28: 	items := []TmuxBrowserItem{
29: 		{Name: "kas_a", Title: "a", Created: time.Now()},
30: 		{Name: "kas_b", Title: "b", Created: time.Now()},
31: 		{Name: "kas_c", Title: "c", Created: time.Now()},
32: 	}
33: 	b := NewTmuxBrowserOverlay(items)
34:

36: 
37: 	b.HandleKey(tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyDown})
38: 	assert.Equal(t, 1, b.selectedIdx)
39: 
40: 	b.HandleKey(tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyDown})
41: 	assert.Equal(t, 2, b.selectedIdx)
42: 
43: 	b.HandleKey(tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyUp})
44: 	assert.Equal(t, 1, b.selectedIdx)
45: }

53: 	assert.Len(t, b.filtered, 2)
54:

56: 	// so we use "d" then "b" (non-action-key characters) to enter search mode safely.
57: 	b.HandleKey(tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyRunes, Runes: []rune("d")})
58: 	b.HandleKey(tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyRunes, Runes: []rune("b")})

78: 		{"o attaches when search empty", tea.KeyMsg{Type: tea.KeyRunes, Runes: []rune("o")}, true, "attach"},
79: 	}

metadata.preview:
// View implements Overlay. Returns the rendered overlay string.
func (f *FormOverlay) View() string {
	w := f.width
	if w < 40 {
		w = 40
	}

	titleStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
		Foreground(colorIris).
		Bold(true).
		MarginBottom(1)

	hintStyle := lipgloss.NewStyle().
		Foreground(colorMuted).
		MarginTop(1)

	content := titleStyle.Render(f.title) + "\n"
	content += f.form.View() + "\n"
	content += hintStyle.Render("tab/↑↓ navigate · enter create")

parts.text:
The ContextMenu stores `x, y` in its struct but never reads them. The Manager's `Render` uses `m.x, m.y` for `PlaceOverlay`. So the x,y in ContextMenu are dead fields. This is minor — the fields are unused but harmless.

parts.text:
I see a subtle issue in the "kill" case: after `HandleKey` returns `Dismissed: true`, the Manager has already cleared the overlay. But then `browser.RemoveSelected()` is called on the captured pointer, and `browser.IsEmpty()` is checked. If the browser is empty, `m.overlays.Dismiss()` is called — but the overlay was already dismissed by `HandleKey`. This is fine because:
1. The `browser` pointer is still valid (Go GC keeps it alive)
2. `RemoveSelected()` modifies the browser's internal state (which is fine even though it's no longer the active overlay)
3. `m.overlays.Dismiss()` is a no-op since the overlay is already nil

Wait — actually there's a problem here. In the "kill" case, if the browser is NOT empty after removing the selected item, the overlay should remain visible so the user can kill more sessions. But `HandleKey` already dismissed the overlay (because the "k" key returned `Result{Dismissed: true, Action: "kill"}`). So after killing one session, the browser disappears even if there are more sessions.

parts.text:
This is a **critical issue**. The "kill" action returns `Dismissed: true`, which causes the Manager to clear the overlay. But the old code kept the browser open after killing a session (only closing it if the browser became empty). The new code dismisses the browser on every kill action, breaking the multi-kill workflow.

Let me check the old behavior by looking at what the old `HandleKeyPress` returned:

state.title: Check old tmux browser HandleKeyPress
Check old handleTmuxBrowserAction

parts.text:
Confirmed. In the old code, `BrowserKill` was returned by `HandleKeyPress` but the browser was NOT dismissed — it stayed open. The old `handleTmuxBrowserAction` only set `m.tmuxBrowser = nil` if `m.tmuxBrowser.IsEmpty()`. The browser stayed open for multi-kill.

In the new code, `HandleKey` returns `Result{Dismissed: true, Action: "kill"}`, which causes the Manager to auto-clear the overlay. The browser is always dismissed on kill, breaking multi-kill.

This is a **critical regression**. The fix is to make the "kill" action NOT set `Dismissed: true` — it should return `Result{Action: "kill"}` without dismissing. But then the Manager won't auto-clear, and the app layer needs to handle the non-dismissed action. Actually, looking more carefully at the app layer:

parts.text:
The app layer only calls `handleTmuxBrowserAction` when `result.Dismissed` is true. And `handleTmuxBrowserAction` for the "kill" case tries to keep the browser open if it's not empty — but the Manager already cleared the overlay. So the browser disappears after every kill.

Similarly, the "adopt" and "attach" actions also set `Dismissed: true`, which is correct for those since they should close the browser.

The fix needs to be in `tmuxBrowserOverlay.go`: the "kill" action should NOT set `Dismissed: true`. And the app layer needs to handle non-dismissed actions from the browser. This is a coder-level fix, not a self-fix.

Let me also check if there's a similar issue with the "adopt" action blocking managed items — in the old code, pressing "a" on a managed item returned `BrowserNone` (no action, browser stays open). In the new code, it returns `Result{}` (no dismiss, no action). That's correct.

parts.text:
So `pickerOverlay.go` and `contextMenu.go` use `DefaultStyles()` correctly. But `formOverlay.go`, `permissionOverlay.go`, and `tmuxBrowserOverlay.go` do NOT use `DefaultStyles()` — they create inline styles or use package-level vars. This is inconsistent with the plan's stated goal.

Now let me compile my findings. Let me also quickly check if there are any issues with the `PickerOverlay` not having a `styles` field (it calls `DefaultStyles()` in `View()` every time):

The `PickerOverlay` and `ContextMenu` call `DefaultStyles()` in their `View()` method on every render. This allocates a new `Styles` struct each frame. The other overlays (`ConfirmationOverlay`, `TextOverlay`, `TextInputOverlay`) store `styles` in a field set at construction time. This inconsistency is minor — `DefaultStyles()` is cheap (just struct construction), but it's a style inconsistency.

Let me now write the signal file. Let me summarize my findings:

**Critical:**
1. TmuxBrowserOverlay "kill" action regression — browser dismisses after every kill, breaking multi-kill workflow

**Important:**
2. `formOverlay.go`, `permissionOverlay.go`, and `tmuxBrowserOverlay.go` don't use `DefaultStyles()` — inconsistent with plan goal and other overlays
3. `tmuxBrowserOverlay.go` retains 7 package-level `var` style declarations that the plan says should be removed
4. Dead `x, y` fields in `ContextMenu` struct — stored but never read

**Minor:**
5. Redundant `m.overlays.Dismiss()` calls after `HandleKey` returns `Dismissed: true` in ClickUp handlers

-# Rewrite Overlay UI Implementation Plan
-
-**Goal:** Clean-room rewrite of `ui/overlay/` to replace 8 ad-hoc overlay structs (each with different APIs) with a unified `Overlay` interface and `OverlayManager`. Eliminates the 27-state switch explosion in `app/app_input.go` and the 20-case render switch in `app/app.go:View()` by making overlays polymorphic. Preserves all existing overlay types and their behavior.
-
-**Architecture:** A new `Overlay` interface defines the contract: `HandleKey(tea.KeyMsg) Result`, `View() string`, `SetSize(w, h int)`. The `Result` type carries dismiss/submit/action signals so the `home` struct doesn't need per-overlay type assertions. An `OverlayManager` holds the active overlay stack (modal + toast layer), handles `PlaceOverlay` compositing, and exposes `IsActive() bool` for the input guard. Each concrete overlay (confirmation, text input, picker, form, permission, context menu, tmux browser) is rewritten to implement the interface. The `home` struct replaces 8 pointer fields with one `OverlayManager`, and the giant state/render switches collapse to `if m.overlays.IsActive() { return m.overlays.HandleKey(msg) }`.

parts.text: Now let me write the signal file. The critical issue (tmux browser kill regression) and the important issues (inconsistent style usage) need coder attention:

input.content:
Round 1 — changes required.

## critical

- `ui/overlay/tmuxBrowserOverlay.go:226` — "kill" action returns `Result{Dismissed: true, Action: "kill"}`, which causes the Manager to auto-clear the overlay. In the old code, `HandleKeyPress` returned `BrowserKill` without dismissing — the browser stayed open so the user could kill multiple sessions. Now the browser closes after every kill, breaking the multi-kill workflow. The fix: "kill" should return `Result{Action: "kill"}` (without `Dismissed: true`), and the app layer (`app/app_input.go:1021-1033`) needs to handle non-dismissed action results from the browser. Same pattern applies to "adopt" and "attach" which correctly dismiss, but the app layer's `if result.Dismissed` guard means non-dismissed actions are silently dropped.

## important

- `ui/overlay/formOverlay.go:183-200` — `View()` creates inline `lipgloss.NewStyle()` calls for title, hint, and border styles instead of using `DefaultStyles()`. The plan explicitly states "All three use `DefaultStyles()` for their border/title/hint rendering instead of inline `lipgloss.NewStyle()` calls." This is inconsistent with `pickerOverlay.go` and `contextMenu.go` which correctly use `DefaultStyles()`.

- `ui/overlay/permissionOverlay.go:67-94` — `render()` creates 7 inline style definitions instead of using `DefaultStyles()`. The permission overlay has a unique gold title color (not iris), so `DefaultStyles()` may need a `WarningTitle` style or the overlay can use `styles.WarningBorder` + a local title override. Either way, the current approach duplicates what `Styles` was designed to consolidate.

- `ui/overlay/tmuxBrowserOverlay.go:27-57` — 7 package-level `var` style declarations (`browserBorderStyle`, `browserTitleStyle`, `browserSearchStyle`, `browserItemStyle`, `browserSelectedStyle`, `browserMutedStyle`, `browserHintStyle`) remain. The plan Task 7 Step 3 says "Remove package-level `var` style declarations ... now served by `DefaultStyles()`". These should be replaced with `DefaultStyles()` calls in `render()`, matching the pattern used by `pickerOverlay.go` and `contextMenu.go`.

## self-fixed

- `ui/overlay/contextMenu.go:18-25` — removed dead `x, y` fields from `ContextMenu` struct (position now managed by `Manager.ShowPositioned`). Updated `NewContextMenu` signature to remove unused `x, y` params, updated all callers.
- `app/app_input.go:938,944,964,972,1007,1014` — removed redundant `m.overlays.Dismiss()` calls in ClickUp handlers that fired after `HandleKey` already auto-cleared the overlay via `Result{Dismissed: true}`.
